# stripe_source (Resource)

`Source` objects allow you to accept a variety of payment methods. They
represent a customer's payment instrument, and can be used with the Stripe API
just like a `Card` object: once chargeable, they can be charged, or can be
attached to customers.

Stripe doesn't recommend using the deprecated [Sources API](https://docs.stripe.com/api/sources).
We recommend that you adopt the [PaymentMethods API](https://docs.stripe.com/api/payment_methods).
This newer API provides access to our latest features and payment method types.

Related guides: [Sources API](https://docs.stripe.com/sources) and [Sources & Customers](https://docs.stripe.com/sources/customers).



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

> **NOTE**: [Write-only arguments](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments) are supported in Terraform 1.11 and later.

- `amount` (Number) A positive integer in the smallest currency unit (that is, 100 cents for $1.00, or 1 for ÂĄ1, Japanese Yen being a zero-decimal currency) representing the total amount associated with the source. This is the amount for which the source will be chargeable once ready. Required for `single_use` sources.
- `currency` (String) Three-letter [ISO code for the currency](https://stripe.com/docs/currencies) associated with the source. This is the currency for which the source will be chargeable once ready. Required for `single_use` sources.
- `customer` (String) The ID of the customer to which this source is attached. This will not be present when the source has not been attached to a customer.
- `flow` (String) The authentication `flow` of the source. `flow` is one of `redirect`, `receiver`, `code_verification`, `none`.
- `mandate` (Attributes, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Information about a mandate possibility attached to a source object (generally for bank debits) as well as its acceptance status. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandate))
- `metadata` (Map of String) Set of [key-value pairs](https://docs.stripe.com/api/metadata) that you can attach to an object. This can be useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured format.
- `original_source` (String, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) The source to share.
- `owner` (Attributes) Information about the owner of the payment instrument that may be used or required by particular source types. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--owner))
- `receiver` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--receiver))
- `redirect` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--redirect))
- `source_order` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--source_order))
- `statement_descriptor` (String) Extra information about a source. This will appear on your customer's statement every time you charge the source.
- `token` (String, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) An optional token used to create the source. When passed, token properties will override source parameters.
- `type` (String) The `type` of the source. The `type` is a payment method, one of `ach_credit_transfer`, `ach_debit`, `alipay`, `bancontact`, `card`, `card_present`, `eps`, `giropay`, `ideal`, `multibanco`, `klarna`, `p24`, `sepa_debit`, `sofort`, `three_d_secure`, or `wechat`. An additional hash is included on the source with a name matching this value. It contains additional information specific to the [payment method](https://docs.stripe.com/sources) used.
- `usage` (String) Either `reusable` or `single_use`. Whether this source should be reusable or not. Some source types may or may not be reusable by construction, while others may leave the option at creation. If an incompatible value is passed, an error will be returned.

### Read-Only

- `ach_credit_transfer` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--ach_credit_transfer))
- `ach_debit` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--ach_debit))
- `acss_debit` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--acss_debit))
- `alipay` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--alipay))
- `allow_redisplay` (String) This field indicates whether this payment method can be shown again to its customer in a checkout flow. Stripe products such as Checkout and Elements use this field to determine whether a payment method can be shown as a saved payment method in a checkout flow. The field defaults to â€śunspecifiedâ€ť.
- `au_becs_debit` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--au_becs_debit))
- `bancontact` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--bancontact))
- `card` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--card))
- `card_present` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--card_present))
- `client_secret` (String) The client secret of the source. Used for client-side retrieval using a publishable key.
- `code_verification` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--code_verification))
- `created` (Number) Time at which the object was created. Measured in seconds since the Unix epoch.
- `eps` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--eps))
- `giropay` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--giropay))
- `id` (String) Unique identifier for the object.
- `ideal` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--ideal))
- `klarna` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--klarna))
- `livemode` (Boolean) If the object exists in live mode, the value is `true`. If the object exists in test mode, the value is `false`.
- `multibanco` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--multibanco))
- `object` (String) String representing the object's type. Objects of the same type share the same value.
- `p24` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--p24))
- `sepa_credit_transfer` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--sepa_credit_transfer))
- `sepa_debit` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--sepa_debit))
- `sofort` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--sofort))
- `status` (String) The status of the source, one of `canceled`, `chargeable`, `consumed`, `failed`, or `pending`. Only `chargeable` sources can be used to create a charge.
- `three_d_secure` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--three_d_secure))
- `wechat` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--wechat))

<a id="nestedatt--mandate"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandate`

Optional:

- `acceptance` (Attributes, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) The parameters required to notify Stripe of a mandate acceptance or refusal by the customer. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandate--acceptance))
- `amount` (Number, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) The amount specified by the mandate. (Leave null for a mandate covering all amounts)
- `currency` (String, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) The currency specified by the mandate. (Must match `currency` of the source)
- `interval` (String, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) The interval of debits permitted by the mandate. Either `one_time` (just permitting a single debit), `scheduled` (with debits on an agreed schedule or for clearly-defined events), or `variable`(for debits with any frequency)
- `notification_method` (String, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) The method Stripe should use to notify the customer of upcoming debit instructions and/or mandate confirmation as required by the underlying debit network. Either `email` (an email is sent directly to the customer), `manual` (a `source.mandate_notification` event is sent to your webhooks endpoint and you should handle the notification) or `none` (the underlying debit network does not require any notification).

<a id="nestedatt--mandate--acceptance"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandate.acceptance`

Required:

- `status` (String, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) The status of the mandate acceptance. Either `accepted` (the mandate was accepted) or `refused` (the mandate was refused).

Optional:

- `date` (Number, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) The Unix timestamp (in seconds) when the mandate was accepted or refused by the customer.
- `ip` (String, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) The IP address from which the mandate was accepted or refused by the customer.
- `offline` (Attributes, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) The parameters required to store a mandate accepted offline. Should only be set if `mandate[type]` is `offline` (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandate--acceptance--offline))
- `online` (Attributes, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) The parameters required to store a mandate accepted online. Should only be set if `mandate[type]` is `online` (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandate--acceptance--online))
- `type` (String, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) The type of acceptance information included with the mandate. Either `online` or `offline`
- `user_agent` (String, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) The user agent of the browser from which the mandate was accepted or refused by the customer.

<a id="nestedatt--mandate--acceptance--offline"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandate.acceptance.offline`

Required:

- `contact_email` (String, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) An email to contact you with if a copy of the mandate is requested, required if `type` is `offline`.


<a id="nestedatt--mandate--acceptance--online"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandate.acceptance.online`

Optional:

- `date` (Number, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) The Unix timestamp (in seconds) when the mandate was accepted or refused by the customer.
- `ip` (String, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) The IP address from which the mandate was accepted or refused by the customer.
- `user_agent` (String, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) The user agent of the browser from which the mandate was accepted or refused by the customer.




<a id="nestedatt--owner"></a>
### Nested Schema for `owner`

Optional:

- `address` (Attributes) Owner's address. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--owner--address))
- `email` (String) Owner's email address.
- `name` (String) Owner's full name.
- `phone` (String) Owner's phone number (including extension).

Read-Only:

- `verified_address` (Attributes) Verified owner's address. Verified values are verified or provided by the payment method directly (and if supported) at the time of authorization or settlement. They cannot be set or mutated. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--owner--verified_address))
- `verified_email` (String) Verified owner's email address. Verified values are verified or provided by the payment method directly (and if supported) at the time of authorization or settlement. They cannot be set or mutated.
- `verified_name` (String) Verified owner's full name. Verified values are verified or provided by the payment method directly (and if supported) at the time of authorization or settlement. They cannot be set or mutated.
- `verified_phone` (String) Verified owner's phone number (including extension). Verified values are verified or provided by the payment method directly (and if supported) at the time of authorization or settlement. They cannot be set or mutated.

<a id="nestedatt--owner--address"></a>
### Nested Schema for `owner.address`

Optional:

- `city` (String) City, district, suburb, town, or village.
- `country` (String) Two-letter country code ([ISO 3166-1 alpha-2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-2)).
- `line1` (String) Address line 1, such as the street, PO Box, or company name.
- `line2` (String) Address line 2, such as the apartment, suite, unit, or building.
- `postal_code` (String) ZIP or postal code.
- `state` (String) State, county, province, or region ([ISO 3166-2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-2)).


<a id="nestedatt--owner--verified_address"></a>
### Nested Schema for `owner.verified_address`

Read-Only:

- `city` (String) City, district, suburb, town, or village.
- `country` (String) Two-letter country code ([ISO 3166-1 alpha-2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-2)).
- `line1` (String) Address line 1, such as the street, PO Box, or company name.
- `line2` (String) Address line 2, such as the apartment, suite, unit, or building.
- `postal_code` (String) ZIP or postal code.
- `state` (String) State, county, province, or region ([ISO 3166-2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-2)).



<a id="nestedatt--receiver"></a>
### Nested Schema for `receiver`

Optional:

- `refund_attributes_method` (String) Type of refund attribute method, one of `email`, `manual`, or `none`.

Read-Only:

- `address` (String) The address of the receiver source. This is the value that should be communicated to the customer to send their funds to.
- `amount_charged` (Number) The total amount that was moved to your balance. This is almost always equal to the amount charged. In rare cases when customers deposit excess funds and we are unable to refund those, those funds get moved to your balance and show up in amount_charged as well. The amount charged is expressed in the source's currency.
- `amount_received` (Number) The total amount received by the receiver source. `amount_received = amount_returned + amount_charged` should be true for consumed sources unless customers deposit excess funds. The amount received is expressed in the source's currency.
- `amount_returned` (Number) The total amount that was returned to the customer. The amount returned is expressed in the source's currency.
- `refund_attributes_status` (String) Type of refund attribute status, one of `missing`, `requested`, or `available`.


<a id="nestedatt--redirect"></a>
### Nested Schema for `redirect`

Required:

- `return_url` (String) The URL you provide to redirect the customer to after they authenticated their payment.

Read-Only:

- `failure_reason` (String) The failure reason for the redirect, either `user_abort` (the customer aborted or dropped out of the redirect flow), `declined` (the authentication failed or the transaction was declined), or `processing_error` (the redirect failed due to a technical error). Present only if the redirect status is `failed`.
- `status` (String) The status of the redirect, either `pending` (ready to be used by your customer to authenticate the transaction), `succeeded` (successful authentication, cannot be reused) or `not_required` (redirect should not be used) or `failed` (failed authentication, cannot be reused).
- `url` (String) The URL provided to you to redirect a customer to as part of a `redirect` authentication flow.


<a id="nestedatt--source_order"></a>
### Nested Schema for `source_order`

Optional:

- `items` (Attributes List) List of items constituting the order. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--source_order--items))
- `shipping` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--source_order--shipping))

Read-Only:

- `amount` (Number) A positive integer in the smallest currency unit (that is, 100 cents for $1.00, or 1 for ÂĄ1, Japanese Yen being a zero-decimal currency) representing the total amount for the order.
- `currency` (String) Three-letter [ISO currency code](https://www.iso.org/iso-4217-currency-codes.html), in lowercase. Must be a [supported currency](https://stripe.com/docs/currencies).
- `email` (String) The email address of the customer placing the order.

<a id="nestedatt--source_order--items"></a>
### Nested Schema for `source_order.items`

Optional:

- `amount` (Number) The amount (price) for this order item.
- `currency` (String) This currency of this order item. Required when `amount` is present.
- `description` (String) Human-readable description for this order item.
- `parent` (String) The ID of the associated object for this line item. Expandable if not null (e.g., expandable to a SKU).
- `quantity` (Number) The quantity of this order item. When type is `sku`, this is the number of instances of the SKU to be ordered.
- `type` (String) The type of this order item. Must be `sku`, `tax`, or `shipping`.


<a id="nestedatt--source_order--shipping"></a>
### Nested Schema for `source_order.shipping`

Required:

- `address` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--source_order--shipping--address))

Optional:

- `carrier` (String) The delivery service that shipped a physical product, such as Fedex, UPS, USPS, etc.
- `name` (String) Recipient name.
- `phone` (String) Recipient phone (including extension).
- `tracking_number` (String) The tracking number for a physical product, obtained from the delivery service. If multiple tracking numbers were generated for this purchase, please separate them with commas.

<a id="nestedatt--source_order--shipping--address"></a>
### Nested Schema for `source_order.shipping.address`

Required:

- `line1` (String) Address line 1, such as the street, PO Box, or company name.

Optional:

- `city` (String) City, district, suburb, town, or village.
- `country` (String) Two-letter country code ([ISO 3166-1 alpha-2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-2)).
- `line2` (String) Address line 2, such as the apartment, suite, unit, or building.
- `postal_code` (String) ZIP or postal code.
- `state` (String) State, county, province, or region ([ISO 3166-2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-2)).

<a id="nestedatt--code_verification"></a>
### Nested Schema for `code_verification`

Read-Only:

- `attempts_remaining` (Number) The number of attempts remaining to authenticate the source object with a verification code.
- `status` (String) The status of the code verification, either `pending` (awaiting verification, `attempts_remaining` should be greater than 0), `succeeded` (successful verification) or `failed` (failed verification, cannot be verified anymore as `attempts_remaining` should be 0).
